observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

instruct the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all valid expenditures chargeable to the QRF and Mitigation Fund-MOOE are obligated at year-end, to promptly determine the unutilized balances of the fund; and

ensure that all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) – MOOE are transferred to the Special Trust Fund, in compliance with Section 21 of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 10121 and Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: Unexpended balance for CY 2023 and CY 2024 were not yet transferred due to the death of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

require the Inventory Committee, GSO and Accounting Office to reconcile the Office Supplies Inventory balance per Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), stock cards and supplies ledger cards and effect the necessary adjustments to present fairly the balances presented for Inventories accounts; and 

require the Inventory Committee to include in the physical count of inventories the Drugs and Medicines Inventory to properly account all inventories of LGU Tuao.
observations.reason:
The concerned offices are still on the process of establishing their respective records.




Drugs and Medicines Inventory were still not included in the physical count.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

require the Accounting Office and the GSO to update the PPELC and PC, respectively, to properly reconcile their records;


assess whether the created Inventory Committee has adequate number of members who shall plan/strategize and undertake the physical count of all PPEs of the LGU and increase their numbers, whenever necessary and practicable; and

study the applicability of COA Circular No. 2020-006 which provides the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of properties for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, to come-up with a complete RPCPPE that will help both the Accounting Office and the GSO in updating and reconciling their records; thus, establishing the fair presentation of the PPE account in the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Updating of the PPELC and PC by the Accounting and GSO, respectively, is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to provide adequate disclosure of necessary information as to details, nature, descriptions and breakdown of amounts in the Notes to Financial Statements for a more comprehensive, complete and understandable information on the financial condition and status of operations of the LGU and to ensure transparency and/or to achieve fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: There are still needed disclosures in the notes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

ensure that all identified development projects embodied in the Annual Investment Program are supported with concrete plans and designs, minimizing any issues and procurement-and-implementation-ready, including conduct of detailed engineering for infrastructure projects, in order to complete them within the target completion date to maximize the benefit for its intended beneficiaries;

prepare a holistic and comprehensive but realistic catch-up plan, after conducting proper monitoring and follow-up activities, to expedite the project implementation of PPAs funded especially from prior years’ appropriation, which are still at the agency pipeline, to reduce backlogs; and

consider the need for reprogramming of the PPAs from prior years’ appropriations, where necessary, after determination of a more relevant and responsive and procurement-and-implementation-ready projects that may be included under the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality in adherence with applicable laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: There were still projects which were not implemented within the target completion date. Reiterated

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through the MDRRMO together with the concerned implementing offices, enforce the full implementation of the unimplemented PPAs, by developing a catch-up plan, to fully support the objectives of the disaster risk management activities of the LDRRM Council.

We also recommended that Management, in the event that said PPAs shall be determined to be not viable after the conduct of close monitoring and evaluation, consider the reprogramming of PPAs and prepare and submit an amended LDRRMFIP for approval by the Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: There were still projects which were not implemented within the target completion date. Reiterated

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP annually following the prescribed format, as shown in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for the effective monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of LDRRM Fund.
observations.reason: LDRRMFIP for CY 2024 was still not prepared. Reiterated

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to require the Collecting Officers to remit all collections on a daily basis and ensure that all collections remitted to him are deposited daily/regularly and intact to the authorized depository bank, in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the Local Treasury Manual.
observations.reason: Collecting Officers still do not remit all collections on a daily basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the BAC to comply with the requirement on the submission of APPs, PMRs, and APCPI Results to the GPPB pursuant to applicable provisions of GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020, in order to enable the Municipality to achieve its performance targets and to avert any risk of possible sanctions due to such non-compliance.
observations.reason: Posting of required reports will be done after preparation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Disposal Committee to facilitate the proper and timely disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and EO No. 309 to avoid further deterioration of the PPE, and to generate revenue before the appraised value of these properties significantly decrease.
observations.reason: Actual disposal of unserviceable properties was not yet undertaken due to failure of bidding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through all concerned offices, ensure the fair presentation of expenses in its financial statements by:
requiring all offices to use appropriate expense accounts in their budget proposals, ensuring that budget submissions align with government accounting and budgeting standards and accurately reflect the nature and purpose of the expenses, thereby preventing misclassification at the budgeting stage;
strengthening the review process of budget proposals by the MBO through careful evaluation of the accounts used in the proposals, ensuring they correspond to the correct expense classifications before finalizing the budget; and
moving forward, ensure that all MOOE transactions are properly classified in the specific or appropriate MOOE account, accurately reflecting their true nature and purpose.
Management’s Comment/s:  The Management agreed to comply with the audit recommendation. During the exit conference, the MBO mentioned that copy of the RCA for LGUs were already furnished to the different offices of the LGU which will be used as their reference in the preparation of their respective budget proposal.

We recommended that Management:
require the Municipal Accountant to record all procured supplies as asset, under inventory accounts, upon receipt and acceptance and only record the same as expense once issued to the end-user to comply with the provisions of the MNGAS, to facilitate fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and
require the concerned GSO staff to prepare the SSMI and submit the same to the Accounting Office to facilitate recording of the issuance of inventory to the appropriate expense account.
Management’s Comment/s:  The Management accepts the recommendation and agreed to comply. That, physical count of the existing inventories will be conducted to properly adjust the balance in the books.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to properly recognize the semi-expendable items issued during the year and in the prior years, to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
Management’s Comment/s:  The Management accepts the recommendation and committed to make the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended that Management evaluate the collectability of these receivables, intensify collection efforts on the past-due accounts and consider the initiation of legal remedies for accounts that remain unpaid despite the issuance of demand letters, in order to safeguard government funds and uphold fiscal accountability.
Management’s Comment/s:  The Management accepts the recommendation and committed to initiate again the collection of the outstanding loan receivables.

We recommended Management to facilitate fair presentation of accounts in the financial statement and instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that future advance payments should be recorded under Advances to Contractors account and only transferred to CIP accounts upon recoupment.
Management’s Comment/s:  The Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation in the proper recording of advance payments to contractors.

We recommended that Management:
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to initiate the immediate transfer of the SEF collections from the GF bank account to the SEF bank account in compliance with Section 272 of RA No. 7160; and
establish a regular fund transfer schedule and implement monitoring controls to ensure timely release and proper SEF utilization ensuring delivery of basic educational services to constituents.
Management’s Comment/s:  The Management agreed to comply with the recommendation in the transferring the SEF collection to the SEF bank account and to establish a regular fund transfer schedule as soon as practicable.

We recommend that Management:
ensure the timely utilization of LDRRMF for disaster risk reduction and management activities, through proper planning and monitoring of implementation, in order to improve the LGU’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats brought about by various calamities and disasters; and
require the concerned personnel to prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan annually following the prescribed format, as shown in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for the effective monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of LDRRMF.
Management’s Comment/s:  The Management accepted the recommendations and committed that it will closely monitor and supervise the implementation of the projects funded under the LDRRMF. Management also committed to prepare the LDRRMFIP annually.

We recommended that the Management;
maximize the utilization of the 20% DF by expediting the implementation of the pending PPAs and identify causes of non-implementation and to develop feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement developmental projects for the attainment of the socio-economic objectives of these PPAs; and
to exercise prudence, diligence, and precision, based on available data, in evaluating and monitoring the progress of each project to ensure that objectives are achieved within the targeted timeframe and to optimize the utilization of its funds.
Management’s Comment/s:  The Management committed to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accountant:

to prepare the adjusting journal entries to reclassify the cost of completed projects recorded in the CIP to the appropriate PPE accounts and to take up the unrecognized depreciation, to facilitate fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements;

to include disclosure in the Notes to FS on the useful life to be used for the depreciation of Infrastructure Assets to enable transparency and reliability of the information provided in the financial statements; and

to ensure compliance with Section 50 of the MNGAS on the proper reclassification of all completed projects to their appropriate PPE accounts, to avoid misstatements in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were already made

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned LGU Officials to: 

evaluate the recording of the utilization of funds received from NGAs and other LGUs to record the necessary adjusting entries and, moving forward, ensure the proper recording of financial transactions in the books, particularly on the receipts, utilizations and liquidations of fund transfers, for the fair presentation of the Due to NGAs account in the financial statements; and

cause the immediate submission of liquidation reports to the source agencies to facilitate proper recording of transactions in the Source Agencies’ books of accounts.

Adjustments were already made





Liquidation reports of the subject FTs were all submitted to the SA.

observations.recommendation: We commended Management for the optimal utilization of the fund received under the LGSF-SBDP of the NFT-ELCAC for its intended purpose and in adhering with the guidelines set forth under DBM Local Budget Circular No. 151 dated March 30, 2023.

We recommended that Management, for future contracts, instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct the Pre-bid Conference, for infrastructure projects with ABC of P1,000,000.00 or more, within the required time prescribed under Section 22 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the complete supporting documents for the 25 infrastructure projects, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and to facilitate conduct of review and evaluation by the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), through the BAC Secretariat, to ensure that before signing of the contract, all documents forming part thereof including the CSHP approved by the DOLE should have been submitted by the winning bidder pursuant to Section 37.2.3 (f) of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management, for future contracts, instruct the BAC to require the winning bidder to submit/post the required Performance Security prior to the signing of contract in compliance with the provisions of the RIRR of RA No. 9184, to fully protect the interest of the LGU in case the supplier fails to comply with the terms and conditions of the contract; otherwise, disqualify the said bidder for failure to post a Performance Security and declare the bidding a failure as stipulated in the Notice of Award.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC to include in the RFQ specific information for the submission of required eligibility documents and the deadline for its submission, instructions to bidders and other pertinent terms and conditions of the contract, pursuant to Annex H of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Optimally use the allocation of the DRRMF, through the full implementation of the projects and activities allowed and mentioned in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1, to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks; and

Direct the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to monitor the extent of the implementation of the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the public receive the benefits that may accrue from the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The LGU mainly concentrated and devoted the implementation of the fund on the observance and implementation of protocols set forth by the IATF in response to the COVID-19.
The subsidiary ledgers were already submitted and the unexpended balances were already transferred.  The disclosures needed were also made and the report to different offices were already submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Examine the reasons for the increase in the number of job order personnel and the need to hire such personnel, especially those performing clerical and administrative functions.  Hiring of personnel under job order contract should only be resorted to as the need arises and deployed to offices that actually are in need of their services; and
Formulate Policy Guidelines in hiring contractual/job order personnel and ensure that the policy guidelines formulated do not, in any way, restrict the manpower requirements needed in the delivery of vital services for the implementation of the various programs and activities of the Municipality and will promote optimum utilization of financial resources.
observations.managementAction: The vacant positions were already published and the hiring of qualified applicants is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:

To require the Accounting Office to strictly observe the submission of the payroll prior to the processing of the cash advances for salaries and to ensure that the amount of DV for checking shall be equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period;
To instruct the Cashier-Designate to ensure proper disbursement of cash on pay day, and refund immediately any excess of his cash advances; and

Moving forward, for the LGU to consider embracing the automation of its payroll system in order to be adoptive in the changes of time. The automation of the payroll system ensures the efficiency and effectiveness of the payment of salaries to its employees. It is very simple, affordable and inexpensive considering the proximity of one of its authorized depository banks to the Municipal Hall.
We recommended that the Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% development fund and fast-track the implementation of the unimplemented development projects as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to a special trust fund under the account “Trust Liability – DRRM” as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant fully disclose the noted deficiencies to adequately provide relevant information as regards the Financial Statements. Consequently, the account shall be used to record transfers of the agency’s unutilized QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, receipts of NDRRMF, DRRMF from other LGUs and other sources.

We further recommended that subsidiary ledgers for the transfer of the agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund be maintained.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements of all its depository bank accounts, take up reconciling items, and effect adjustment in the GL to ensure the correct balance of the Cash in Bank accounts; and
Exert more time and effort to trace, verify and reconcile regularly the book balances against the bank records, including reconciliation with the cashbook maintained by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Determine the details of each accounts under the Inter-Agency Payables account by segregating those balances that pertain to his period with those amounts that were only carried over for easy establishment of its subsidiary ledgers;
Remit those accounts that are already past due or due for liquidation; and
Henceforth, maintain and submit the ledgers of each account of the Inter-Agency Payables to support their balances in the Financial Statements in accordance with Sections 111 and 114 of PD No.1445.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections daily and intact, and strictly implement controls over collections and remittances to avoid misappropriations, misuse and/or concealment of irregularities in the handling of cash; and
Instruct the Collectors to religiously observe daily remittance of all their collections, together with the issued official receipts, and preparation and submission of the corresponding RCDs to the Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the current portion of the long-term loans payable to the Loans Payable – Current, Domestic account and present these payables under the Current Liabilities category in the financial statements for better presentation of liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position as at year-end/reporting date.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the job orders, uniformed personnel and regular employees,  who received said incentive, to refund the amount given them. Otherwise, officials who approved the grant of said incentive shall be held liable for the amount totalling to P318,000.00; and
Avoid giving incentives to its job order workers and to uniformed personnel assigned in the Municipality, who are not allowed to receive the same, pursuant CSC Resolution No. 020790 dated June 2, 2002 and Items 1 and 6 of CSC Memorandum Circular No.1 s. 2001, so that government funds are spent in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management:
Determine and appropriate the budget needed for the payment of salaries of casual employees in order to avoid the use of appropriations allocated for salaries of permanent employees or any expense as the case may be in accordance with Sections 321 and 336 of the Local Government Code; and
Ensure that future transfer/use of funds be supported by appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget and be transmitted to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review and approval.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the HRMO to require the concerned job order workers to submit their approved DTRs and individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to attach the submitted DTRs and accomplishment reports in the payroll in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Cashier-Designate to liquidate immediately his cash advance, and any excess therein should also be immediately refunded in order to avoid any loss/misuse of the excess cash advance. Also, segregate his personal money from his monetary accountabilities; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the full liquidation of the cash advances made by the Cashier Designate or any accountable officer as the case maybe, before granting another cash advance.
We recommended that the Cashier Designate ensure that the payrolls are duly accomplished and certified by him in order to adhere with the provisions of Item 5.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly ensure that payments for fuel, oil and lubricants are duly supported with properly and completely filled up driver’s trip ticket and billing statements from the supplier for proper evaluation and reasonableness of fuel consumption.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Administrative and Treasury Offices to stop the practice of including loan repayments to the Cooperative as deductions in payrolls.  Likewise, follow strictly Section 262 of GAAM governing salaries deductions and retentions.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and officials concerned to submit the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit;
Henceforth, ensure that financial assistance extended to those participating in the socio-cultural activities be supported with the above-mentioned documentary requirements to provide basis for monitoring the proper use of funds; and
Make sure that fund transfers are recorded in the Due from NGAs account instead of Due to LGUs so that the Municipality can monitor the implementation of the proposed PPAs.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary supporting documents relative to the said transaction, if such documents are actually present, in order for the Audit Team to assess the validity, propriety and legality of the claims; and
All concerned official are enjoined not to approve transaction and disburse government funds without satisfying first the basic documentray requirements provided in COA Cicrcular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. The Municipal Accountant should only process those transactions that are in conformity with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that:
For the current year transactions, Management submit the following:
Monthly summary of leave applications and attendance on regular and special sessions of officials receiving RATA for CY 2019;
Certification from the HRMO that the official or employee did not use government vehicle and is not assigned any government vehicle; and
For regular employees observing an 8-hour work day, a copy of the monthly Daily Time Record (DTR) with supporting Certificates of Appearance and Leave Applications.
For succeeding transactions, Management support the grant of RATA for the ensuing year with the following documents:
For regular officials observing an 8-hour work day, copy of the monthly Daily Time Record (DTR) with supporting Certificates of Appearance and Leave Applications, and a certification from the HRMO that the official or employee did not use government vehicle and is not assigned any government vehicle;
For the members of the Sangguniang Bayan, minutes of the regular sessions and or monthly work schedule attendance or performance standards /Revised Internal Rules of Procedures for CY 2019, and Leave application/certificates of appearance, if applicable; and
Payroll for the payment of RATA should be prepared separately from the payroll for the payment of salaries and wages.

We recommended that the Management:
Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by speeding up the implementation of the prioritized programs, projects and activities funded out of the 20% DF as per Annual Investment Plan and that closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to achieve the desired level of outcomes;
Instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer to immediately look into the unimplemented developmental projects as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan.  Identify causes of non-implementation and develop feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement developmental projects; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very prudent, meticulous, conscientious and precise in evaluating the timely and efficient implementation of each and every project.  Likewise, he should also conduct regular inspection of the projects so that accomplishment is monitored and evaluated based on the project implementation schedule as stated in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Optimally use the allocation of the DRRMF, through the full implementation of the projects and activities allowed and mentioned in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1, to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks; and
Direct the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to monitor the extent of the implementation of the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the public receive the benefits that may accrue from the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report in the form prescribed by the GPPB to be approved and submitted by the Head of Procuring Entity (HoPE) to the GPPB and to this Office in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Management:
Examine the reasons for the increase in the number of job order personnel and the need to hire such personnel, especially those performing clerical and administrative functions.  Hiring of personnel under job order contract should only be resorted to as the need arises and deployed to offices that actually are in need of their services; and
Formulate Policy Guidelines in hiring contractual/job order personnel and ensure that the policy guidelines formulated do not, in any way, restrict the manpower requirements needed in the delivery of vital services for the implementation of the various programs and activities of the Municipality and will promote optimum utilization of financial resources.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee to conduct yearly physical inventory of PPE and submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year. The Committee should also reconcile the report of inventory against the accounting records to determine the correctness of both balances.

We further recommended that Management instruct the Property and Accounting Section to maintain and update the property cards and PPE ledger cards, respectively, and reconcile their records. Likewise, Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) should be issued every time there is an acquisition and transfer of the properties from one accountable officer to another to pinpoint property accountability and responsibility.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted physical inventory of PPE but the report was not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant capitalize the borrowing costs of the construction of the public market as required by Paragraph 18 of IPSAS 5.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant capitalized the interest payments and made necessary adjustments in the appropriate accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that the Municipal Accountant trace the period or date of the Journal Entry Vouchers related to the Road Network account and compute the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of the asset. Prior years’ depreciation should be taken up as Prior Period Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity and current year’s depreciation should be recorded as expense.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already provided for the depreciation expense of the Road Networks.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly signed certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible every beginning of the year for recording purposes; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list from the Office of the Treasurer and ensure that the recorded receivables are based on actual reference documents.
observations.managementAction: Management required the Assessor's Office to submit a certified list showing the name of the tax payers with assessed value for the present and previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose the noted deficiencies in the Notes to Financial Statements of the Municipality as required by IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The disclosure requirements were not fully observed.  Only some noted deficiencies in CY 2018 were disclosed in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose the noted deficiencies in the Notes to Financial Statements as required by IPSAS 20 paragraph 27 to ensure transparency and for the users to be able to fully assess the financial position and performance of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already disclosed the related party transactions in the Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management explain and justify the hiring of legal consultants without the required conformity and concurrence of the Solicitor General and Commission on Audit, respectively, and of other consultants, the services rendered of which were duplication of the functions of the agency regular department heads.  

We also recommended that the Management adhere strictly to the rules and regulations on the procurement of consulting services provided for under COA Circular No. 98-002 dated June 9, 1998 and Section 24.3, IRR-A of RA 9184; and use the prescribed standard contract form for consulting services annexed in the COA-PTTF Contract Review Manual.

Likewise, the Audit Team requires the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the required documents to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality discontinued the hiring of Legal Consultants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management implement the 2016 Salary Standardization Law in compliance with Section 11 of Executive Order No. 201, series of 2016 dated February 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Management already included SSL IV in the Annual Budget for 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from procuring goods and services on a reimbursement basis, except petty purchases to be paid out from the Petty Cash Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management is already avoiding reimbursement basis in the procurement of goods and services.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Petty Cash Officer comply with Section 48 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, especially on the liquidation of the PCF at the end of the year. The Report of Disbursements, together with the Summary of Petty Cash Vouchers (PCVs) paid, and the PCVs with its supporting documents should be submitted on or before the closing of the books of accounts in the Accounting Section. Likewise, all unused petty cash should be strictly deposited to the Municipal Treasurer and the latter to issue the corresponding official receipts.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not allocate a budget for Petty Cash Fund.
The Management already applied for Fidelity Bonds that are sufficient to cover the accountabilities of its Accountable Officers

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management: 

To instruct the Petty Cash Fund Custodian not to allow payment of travelling expenses to be charged to the petty cash fund; 

To require the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the nature of expenses charged against the petty cash fund, and to allow the payment of travelling expenses, irrespective of amount, thru checks or by ADA; and

For future improvement opportunities, to consider evaluating the current amount of the Petty Cash Fund established, and to maintain an amount sufficient only to cover future emergency irregular expenses of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not allocate a budget for Petty Cash Fund.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:

To require the Cashier-Designate to liquidate his cash advances immediately after the pay period;

To instruct the Accounting Office to strictly observe the granting of cash advances only if previous cash advances are properly liquidated; and

To require the Accounting Office to strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances granted to the Cashier-Designate and to submit status of cash advances to the COA on a quarterly basis.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensures that cash advances are properly liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management: 
To instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of allowing student- trainees to perform collection functions; and

To require the Municipal Treasurer to observe proper management of the funds of the LGU by ensuring that collections be performed only by the LGU permanent employees, who are duly designated as Accountable Officers and properly bonded with the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Collection functions were only performed by employees of the Municipality assigned in the Treasury Office.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management: 	
To relieve the Cashier-Designate as one of the Liquidating Officers, and to instruct him to focus on his main responsibility as Disbursing Officer; and

To require the Municipal Treasurer to designate a Liquidating Officer/s only among the collectors or tellers of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office adhered and complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management: 
To instruct the Municipal Treasurer to keep the Accountable Forms, with prioritization on the forms with money value, in a safety vault inside his Office; and

To require the Municipal Treasurer and all other staff of the Treasury Office to adopt control measures to safeguard all the Accountable Forms under their custody.
observations.managementAction:
Safety vault was already procured in the Treasury Office for safekeeping of the accountable forms.

Accountable forms are kept in the safety vault in the Treasury Office.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management: 

To instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly issue Official Receipts and other Accountable Forms in strict numerical sequence; and

To require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms on a monthly basis, and to strictly monitor the issuances of the forms.
observations.managementAction: Official Receipts were issued strictly in numerical sequence.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management: 
To instruct the Field Collectors and Liquidating Officers to issue the correct Accountable Form corresponding to the type of collection; and

To instruct the Municipal Treasurer to check the Report of Collections submitted by the Field Collectors, and to strictly monitor the correctness of the types of Accountable Forms issued by them.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office adhered and complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to Management:

To instruct the Field Collectors to remit their collections daily to their respective Liquidating Officers, who will prepare the Report of Collections and submit the same to the Municipal Treasurer; and

To require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections daily and intact to avoid possibility of theft, mishandling and misuse of the amount collected.
observations.managementAction: Some collections were accumulated and not deposited regularly due to delayed remittance of the field collectors of their collections to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain the full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.   Otherwise, abolish and recast the above items/designations incorporated in the Annual Budget and replace them with items that are necessary for the proper discharge of the functions, targets and programs of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU submit the GAD Plan and Budget and the GAD Accomplishment Report within the time set forth in COA Circular 2014-001 for the team to evaluate the propriety of the utilization of the GAD Fund and to determine if programs and projects included in the plan were implemented by the Municipality for the promotion of gender equality and gender development.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person already submitted the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of IRR of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 and instruct the LDRRM Officer to submit the report within the required period as prescribed in Sec. 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the liquidating officer and all collectors maintain individual cashbook to record their daily collections as prescribed by existing regulations. The use of the cashbook will facilitate monitoring of their respective accountabilities as of a given date.

We strictly recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exercise utmost care in safeguarding the funds of the Municipality in conformity with the provisions of Section 69 (1) of PD 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs by depositing all his collections regularly and intact and post the same in his Cashbook.

We recommended that Management stop the use of the SEF for other purposes and enjoin the Local School Board of Tuao to comply strictly with the law, rules and regulations in the planning, budgeting and utilization of the SEF in order to preclude the occurrence of unauthorized disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer assign a personnel and require to stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers/payrolls and other supporting documents upon payment to avoid reusing of the same for another payment considering the limited resources of the municipality.
